data.title: Judge Strikes Down Biden-Era Rule Capping Credit Card Late Fees At $8
data.description: Federal judge rules against Biden's credit card late fee cap, a win for finance industry. Ruling considered a setback for consumer protection.

data.title: United Airlines To Cut Back On Flights This Summer As It Anticipates Decline In Demand
data.description: United Airlines announced 4% decrease in flight schedule this summer due to lower demand for air travel and retire 21 aircraft.

data.title: US Stock Likely To Open Lower As US-China Tariff War Continues: 'Valuations Are Now Looking More Compelling,' Says Expert
data.description: U.S. stock futures declined on Wednesday following a muted day of trading on Tuesday. Futures of indices were lower in premarket.

data.title: Can AI Talk To Dolphins? Google's DeepMind Creates DolphinGemma To Explore Animal Language
data.description: Google DeepMind has developed DolphinGemma, an AI model designed to mimic and interpret dolphin vocalizations.

data.title: S&P 500 Typically Gains 18% In A Year After VIX Drops From 50 To 30 Level: Here's What Analysts Say
data.description: The S&P 500 has given a 17.9% median return each time the CBOE Volatility Index has fallen from over 50 to the 30 level.
